differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia) secondary to chronic acetaminophen and malnutrition
next_steps: Measure arterial blood gas, serum lactate, and anion gap
Check serum acetaminophen, pyroglutamic acid (5-oxoproline) levels, and serum ketones
Perform renal ultrasound to assess for urinary tract obstruction
Reconcile and discontinue potential causative medications (metformin, acetaminophen)
Obtain arterial ammonia and liver function studies
Continue aggressive fluid resuscitation and hemodynamic monitoring
Perform head CT to rule out structural intracranial pathology

differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia) secondary to chronic acetaminophen and malnutrition
next_steps: Calculate and monitor the anion gap, osmolal gap, and delta-delta ratio
Order serum beta-hydroxybutyrate, serum lactate, and 5-oxoproline (pyroglutamic acid) levels
Perform renal ultrasonography to evaluate for hydronephrosis and obstruction
Obtain urinalysis with microscopy and formal urine/blood cultures
Monitor arterial blood gases to assess pH, pCO2, and severity of respiratory/metabolic compensation
Evaluate for medication-induced adverse effects by reviewing home medication list, specifically discontinuation of metformin and acetaminophen
Perform bedside echocardiography to assess right ventricular function and response to fluid and vasopressor therapy
